A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Special Episode 2: Don’t Stop Networking – Just Do it Differently

March 25, 2020 by Ivan Misner 27 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode
This entry is part 3 of 4 in the series Special Episodes

Synopsis

In this second special episode, Dr. Misner calls on BNI members to find the opportunities in the current crisis by finding focus amidst fear.

  • Focus on what you can do right now.
  • Reach out to your BNI network and ask how you can help.
  • It’s OK to ask for help from your fellow BNI members.
  • Pour your extra time into yourself and your business.
  • Read Seven Tips for Working from Home at IvanMisner.com.
  • Today, people need BNI more than ever.
  • BNI online™ will help you get through this.
  • Everything’s Gonna be OK (See Episode 207).

What will you be doing to cultivate your network in the coming weeks? Share your responses in the comments.

Complete Transcript of Special Episode 2

Hi, this is Ivan Misner, Founder & Chief Visionary Officer of BNI. This is Special Episode #2 of the Official BNI Podcast. Please share this with your fellow BNI members.

The world’s a crazy place right now.

People don’t make good decisions based on fear or anger. There’s plenty of that going on all around the world today. Don’t succumb to it. I don’t know about you, but challenges like this give me an absolute singularity of focus.

In the middle of a serious challenge, it’s hard sometimes to see through the fog that surrounds us.

But there are opportunities. To see those opportunities, you need to have a singular focus on executing a plan. In BNI it’s to transition the organization to BNI online™ (for at least the interim) and not get distracted by noise that doesn’t focus on solutions.

In difficult times, what I’ve seen is one of two things that generally happen to groups of people. They either become frozen in fear or they become focused on solutions.

Fear vs Focus. We cannot change what is going on around us, but we can change how we respond to it. Now is not the time to stop networking – it’s just the time to do it differently for a while.

Keep doing the things you need to do to stay healthy. That’s important, but set aside your fear and focus on what you can do right now to work your way through this very difficult situation.

If you’re like many people, you probably have some free time on your hands. Use it effectively. Activate your BNI network. Reach out to your fellow BNI members and see how they are doing. Find out if there is anything you can do for them. Yesterday, I heard John Maxwell say, “You can’t help everybody, but you can help somebody.” Use this time to help somebody.

And don’t forget, it’s OK for you to ask for help also. The people in your BNI group are your colleagues. They are mostly people that you are at credibility with. It’s OK to share your needs with them.

This is also a great time to pour into yourself. Work on the business plan you intend to execute when this is over. Read books that will stimulate and motivate you to do the things you need to do when this is done. Spend time learning on BNI University (it’s a great time to get some extra CEU’s in). Take a look at a recent blog I wrote on working from home at IvanMisner.com. I’ll include the link in the transcript of the podcast: https://ivanmisner.com/seven-tips-for-working-from-home/.

Most importantly, BNI online™ will help you get through this. You can still invite visitors. They need BNI more than ever! In fact, you might want to go to some BNI Alumni to see if they’d like to come back into the organization. Now’s a great time to expand your network of people.

We’ll get through this. It may not be easy – but we will come out stronger and you’ll be light years ahead of your competition after this is all over. They are shut in and paralyzed. BNI has a plan, a system, and a head start and you are part of that.

This is an opportunity to keep your business going when others don’t have access to the powerful network that you do.

We’re going to come through the other side, and we’ll be a stronger organization when we do. We will be stronger because we are all part of a community that shares a powerful core value of Givers Gain.

Today, people need BNI more than ever. That’s an important part of the messaging that we should share with one another. Today, people need BNI more than ever and BNI Online™ allows you to keep your network strong.

You’re going to have a business tomorrow because of the actions you’re taking and we’re taking today.

Years ago, I had a good friend of mine, Fred Johnson, a singer and speaker, support me at a time of great challenge for the organization as well as for me personally. He heard me speak to the organization earlier at the conference about how we would get through the challenge that existed back then. I handed out buttons to everyone that said EGBOK (Everything’s Going to Be OK). Later that day, he extemporaneously came up with a song about this theme. Everything’s Going to Be OK.

I believe in you and I believe in BNI. And when you come out of this, healthy and well, EGBOK. It may not be easy, but Everything’s Going to Be OK.

Please take a few moments to share with me here on the podcast comments – some of the things you will be doing to cultivate your network over the next several weeks.

Stay healthy and we’ll help you stay successful. This is Ivan Misner, Founder of BNI

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Part of the series Special Episodes Posted in Networking Tagged with BNI online™

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Eva Menon says

    March 25, 2020 at 8:52 am

    I am calling at least 10 of my customers just to check on them. I am on Facebook live daily. I am posting this that help people stay positive and at least for the short term stop thinking corona. I am not asking for orders, not asking for anything that has to do with my business. Only how they are and what I can do to help. I am posting recipes on how to make their own skincare at home. People may not have money now, but keeping up their self-esteem and self-care is impoortant. Once this is over people will think of me when they think of selfcare and skincare and makeup

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 25, 2020 at 10:22 am

      Eva, you are so correct!

      Thanks.

      Ivan

      Reply

    • S Srinivasan says

      March 26, 2020 at 8:41 pm

      Yes it’s true

      Reply

  2. Robyn Nelson says

    March 25, 2020 at 9:05 am

    Thank you Dr Misner for this inspiring podcast. I’m a relatively new member (about 9 months) of the Dream Team here in Wichita Ks. and I’m also Communications Coordinator for our chapter. I have shared this podcast to encourage all our members to listen to it – not only for the value of networking, but also for the encouragement, for the “EGBOK”. So many people find themselves overwhelmed and fearful, this hopefully will encourage them to look past that, and focus on coming out the other side of this situation better and stronger than ever. Thanks again, stay healthy and take care.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 25, 2020 at 10:23 am

      Thanks Robyn, I appreciate you sharing this message with your members.

      Ivan

      Reply

  3. Subaiah SaMuPe says

    March 25, 2020 at 10:28 am

    Yes Sir, We should be connected with business in an effective way, that’s is through Online, We are prepared for the second week’s meeting in online on Friday, This is Subaiah SaMuPe, BNI Dignity Salem, India.

    Reply

  4. Anastasia McGee says

    March 26, 2020 at 4:15 pm

    Dr Ivan Misner,
    I have always wanted to join BNI, finally an awesome friend of mine who insisted now is the time for me to join. I am now a new BNI member in the Tampa Florida (Clearwater chapter), my members are professional, knowledgeable and very supportive.

    As a business owner, some time i felt i was alone in the business, now with this chapter, i have the support and knowledge to grow and establish great friendship with the supportive systems, emphasizing on Core Values.

    Listening to the podcast daily encourages one to stay focus and remain positive especial in these times. This “social distance” with BNI we remain connected online through one to one and listening to the podcast, BNI University and Connect. We have all the tools to grow our business thanks to you the founder.

    Sharing what my mom to me:

    My mom always said to me ” you never stop learning even if you have heard it before”, sometimes the obvious hits you a second time, pass it on, share the knowledge.

    Reply

  5. David Morris says

    March 26, 2020 at 4:37 pm

    We continue to have virtual 1-2-1’s in our chapter with the specific objective of finding actionable ways to promote one another’s business during the “shutdown”. My business continues to grow through BNI referrals and I have been sending out social media posts promoting members in our chapter.

    Reply

  6. Joy Kinsey says

    March 26, 2020 at 4:41 pm

    Great podcast Ivan. I have been with BNI for over 7 years and have meet the most amazing people. They are always there for you. My business has really been hit with the Coronavirus being in Travel. Not sure what will happen but it’s good to know BNI OCEAN VIRW Manly are there to support me. We will come back and better than ever people will want to travel again..
    Thanks again for your great words.

    Reply

  7. Peter Maxwell Reeve says

    March 26, 2020 at 6:27 pm

    Dear Dr Misner,

    Thank you for the energy and passion that you bring to a time of fear and uncertainty for so many people. It is vital that people use our networks for support on every level – emotional, business and surviving, and the elusive power of positivity.

    Whether it is delivering sound advice on a supportive call, or delivering food that you have made to the doorstep of friends and colleagues in lock-down, it is about the community that everyone needs to feel and be a part of.

    We are still operating our design business; part office with appropriate distancing / part remote, and early morning and night we are shopping and making 16 – 20 multiple serve meals, and then delivering them to the doorsteps of friends and colleagues that need them.

    It’s a great feeling of care for them and for us, its not about charity, we believe that those of us who can do – should do!

    Keep Talking, Keep Learning, Keep Growing

    Reply

  8. Rajan Verma says

    March 27, 2020 at 12:19 am

    Lovely Gesture… and I also believe that BNI makes things moving even in this difficult time.

    Reply

  9. Rajan Baliah says

    March 27, 2020 at 1:02 am

    Thanks Dr Ivan Misner

    I agree with you that NOW is the time to keep networking. Networking will help us come out stronger.

    Keep sharing.

    Reply

  10. Glennis Nitafan says

    March 27, 2020 at 1:51 am

    Dear Dr. Misner,

    This is a great reminder especially at this time. While it is challenging, we have to make use of what we have right now to prepare for when it’s time to go back. Sun Tzu says, in times of peace prepare for war, and in times of war prepare for peace. The war we are in is disallows movement but there are no bombs like in the wars that may kill us. Great time to sharpen the saw and take time to recalibrate. God bless thanks!

    Reply

  11. Suresh Pukale says

    March 27, 2020 at 4:03 am

    I have Started My 121s which were long pending due to some reason or the other.
    I have decided to have at lest one 121 daily with either my chapter member or other BNI Member.
    Certainly going to be in touch with my Clients & Labors who are away from the work.
    Thanks Dr. Misner for such a nice Podcast.
    Stay Home Stay Safe.

    Reply

  12. LCCM says

    March 27, 2020 at 4:57 am

    I am a positive person who firmly believes in action, and in making myself useful to people, clients and business partners alike. At this time, we were among the first to move our operations to home office, and to extend online services to all our customers. I’m also reaching out to many (even non customers) to see how we can help.

    All that said, it’s been a very trying time and it’s a challenge to keep spirits high when you don’t know how you’ll be able to pay your next personal bill.

    We’ll keep working and we’ll see how it goes.

    Reply

  13. Edson M . de Moraes says

    March 27, 2020 at 9:08 am

    São dicas de para nós mudarmos , quebrar paradigmas usar ferramenta que BNI esta proporcionando para todos os nembros.
    Obrigado Ivan

    Reply

  14. Gerrard Nguy?n says

    March 30, 2020 at 9:55 am

    Thanks Dr Ivan. I am sharing and translating your message to BNI Vietnam community. You are right, it is hard to find any organization which help enterprise maintain networking like BNI.

    EGBOK !

    Reply

  15. Debra Willis says

    March 30, 2020 at 2:46 pm

    Thank you, I would love to hear the song that Fred Johnson came up with. I am also a musician of sorts and am always interested in inspirational songs.
    My office has been sending videos to all our clients, just to check-in. We also do that daily with our own team. I am a member is the Rainmakers in Richmond, Virginia for the last year. It is a wonderful caring group of professionals that feels like an extended family.
    Thank you for your leadership!

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 30, 2020 at 5:23 pm

      Thanks Debra. I need to find a platform where I can post the MP3 file to link to. This podcast site wouldn’t work well for this. Any suggestions?

      Ivan

      Reply

  16. Anniefe Rodriguez says

    March 31, 2020 at 10:38 pm

    one blessing to thank for despite the hardship- i have more time for learning & be creative in networking activities! i love the positivism of the organization.. my key take aways- EGBOK! ; stay focused on the solutions rather than freeze in fear. THANK YOU VERY MUCH IVAN!!

    Reply

  17. Hemant Mundhra says

    April 2, 2020 at 7:09 am

    I am a day old member of BNI Gladiators chapter in Dubai, UAE. I have to wait for 6 months to see this day. BNI membership is so precious to me. More so, now, with ‘Stay Home Stay Safe’ dictum. I would have been a BNIian in Y2005 but kept postponing and avoiding it. I knew it’s hard work.

    Now, at present, I am mulling my contacts, categorizing them, getting in touch with them. Reigniting, Reestablishing, Reconnecting with them. Finding common ground, knowing about what’s happening around them. Amidst all these, creating quality Invite for my Chapter’s meeting.

    Thank You for this podcast. Will keep listening from your stable.

    Reply

  18. SupTAR / Wholesale and Retailing / Synergy Chapter / BKK says

    April 8, 2020 at 6:50 pm

    It’s a great encouragement and hidden with incentives for BNI’s members to improve themselves by attending CEU training. and also encourage to entrepreneur for surviving then business and changing for thinking and interesting BNI becasue BNI’s is learning organization.

    Reply

  19. Jenny Rodick says

    April 14, 2020 at 7:27 pm

    Ivan – My name is Jenny Rodick and I am President of our little chapter here in Forster Tuncurry NSW Australia (BNI The Lakes). I wanted to share a really positive story about our chapter. We ran our 3rd zoom meeting this morning and had four visitors. One from Wollongong NSW Australia; one from Canberra ACT Australia; one from Italy and one from New Zealand – this would not have been possible without the innovative thinking of BNI Global – thank you – we had a great experience

    Reply

  20. Dr Harsh Udawat says

    April 30, 2020 at 10:45 am

    1). I will continue to take care of patients online, by text messages or audio or video call and in the hospital; performing endoscopy and in-patient care. Sifting to structured tele-medicine soon. I have noticed that simply mentioning “do not worry, you are OK” brings new energy in patients, so I will add this phrase in all my consults. 2) I will call my referral sources and ask them “How are you?”. In fact the line of introduction by Priscilla for Dr Ivan Meisner in the beginning of podcasts ” Hello, Ivan how are you and where are you?” sounds sweet 3) I have taken on to complete 1-2-1’s with all the 113 chapter members afresh, I could do many many more 121’s online prior to lock down ever, and got more referrals per 121 ever. The podcasts have always been great driving force, in particular about the great culture that BNI has. It has changed the way I did business.

    Reply

  21. Tim says

    May 3, 2020 at 12:39 am

    I love your statement that we can’t change our situation outside but we can respond it in different ways and do it right now. Change the ways to build networking. Thanks!

    Reply

  22. Juliette Schatan says

    May 5, 2020 at 12:28 pm

    Hi Ivan! Thanks for this message! Like you I’m one of those people that gets laser focused and becomes a creator of solutions. As a service to my community I’ve created the weekly Zoom meeting Refresh Coffee Time (every SAT@11 PST). Each week we have a different topic related to home improvement and how to deal with being at home at this moment. This week my BNI colleague Andrea will talk about how to transform outdoor spaces. I’m an interior designer and this is what I can do to help people. I’m also working on a series of webinars that will be launched in the next two months. I love giving talks and supporting others make their personal space exactly the way they want it to be?
    Thanks again!

    Reply

  23. Daryl Whicker says

    May 3, 2024 at 8:16 am

    My business grew by leaps and bounds!
    I started networking with people all over the USA and it brings me joy to connect people to referral sources. I love BNI.
    When things happen we just need to be more creative.

    Reply
